summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 10 warning, 10 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: No 🔴 or 🟡 architectural findings. The prior PM review already filed the one 🟡 (`completed > 0` guard fires on partial success).
🟡 test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:155 — `makeExecFn` helper defined but never called by any test; dead test code

🟡 bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:179 — `git checkout HEAD -- .` does not remove untracked files; if the agent creates new files without committing them and the gate fails, those files persist in the worktree after the "revert" (the role spec says commit-or-nothing, making this unlikely in practice, but the revert is semantically incomplete)

The core logic path is correct: merge-base detection → changed-file filtering → agent dispatch → SHA comparison → gate re-run → revert on failure. All 11 edge cases I checked are handled. The two 🟡 findings go to backlog; neither affects production correctness given the agent's instructed commit-or-nothing behavior.

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1503` — Guard `completed > 0` allows the simplify pass to run on partial success (some tasks blocked); spec says "after **all** tasks pass review" — either tighten to `completed > 0 && blocked === 0` or update spec to document partial-success behavior explicitly

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:155` — Dead and broken `makeExecFn` helper (always returns `postSha` for all `rev-parse HEAD` calls, never used by any test); remove it to avoid misleading future contributors

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:165` — No exception guard around `runGateFn` call; if gate throws, revert code at lines 171–185 is never reached and agent changes remain unreversed on the feature branch before the push; add try/catch around the gate call or add a test covering the throw path

The two 🟡 items are worth backlogging: the dead `makeExecFn` test helper is a maintenance smell, and the unguarded `runGateFn` call is a genuine regression risk (though low probability given `runGateInline`'s internal error handling).

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:62` — File paths from `git diff --name-only` are embedded into the agent prompt without sanitization; strip non-printable characters and newlines from each filename before embedding (e.g. `f.replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, "").trim()`) to prevent adversarial filenames from injecting LLM instructions.
No 🔴 critical findings. One 🟡 warning, two 🔵 suggestions.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1578 — `phaseOrder` omits `"simplify"` — add it so simplify-pass token usage appears in the per-phase cost breakdown and progress log (both arrays at lines 1578 and 1604 need updating)

**The 🟡** is a completeness gap introduced by this PR: `setUsageContext("simplify", null)` was added (line 1506) but neither `phaseOrder` array (lines 1578, 1604) was updated to include `"simplify"`, so any token cost from the simplify pass is silently excluded from both the console report and `progress.md`. Goes to backlog.
